key_insight: A two-level machine-learning workflow can map free-energy stability across complex hybrid perovskite alloy compositions, revealing that Sn-based alloys have much narrower stable regions than Pb-based ones.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical design rule for tuning perovskite composition toward high iodide content to improve stability while narrowing the search space for scalable solar-cell materials.
method: Graph neural network interatomic potentials trained on DFT data were used for fast structure relaxations.
Secondary ML models predicted energies directly from unrelaxed alloy structures.
The approach computed free-energy landscapes across composition and phase, including alloy disorder and FA molecular orientations.
evidence_signal: The models were evaluated through free-energy landscape calculations over (Cs/FA)Pb(Br/I)3 and (Cs/FA)Sn(Br/I)3 composition spaces, showing no stabilization near the center and maximum stability at high I content.
watch_item: Because stability depends on accurate treatment of disorder and molecular orientation, the workflow may be sensitive to training-data coverage and transferability outside the sampled composition space.
paper_title: Charting the thermodynamic stability of hybrid perovskite alloys with machine learning
